title: Fixing a Node.js Express Route with Incorrect Route Parameter Validation

statement.markdown: Implement a route parameter validation in a Node.js Express application. The route is designed to accept a string ID as a parameter and return the corresponding user details. However, the current implementation has a logical bug that causes it to fail for certain input types. Fix the bug to ensure the route works correctly for all valid inputs.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a string representing a user ID
statement.outputFormat: The output will be a JSON object containing the user details
statement.notes: The route should return a 404 error for invalid user IDs

examples.explanation: Valid user ID with corresponding details
Invalid user ID with error message

hints.text: Check the data type of the user ID parameter

editorial.title: Route Parameter Validation
editorial.intuition: Use a regular expression to validate the user ID parameter
editorial.bruteForce: Use a try-catch block to catch any errors during route resolution
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a regular expression to validate the user ID parameter and return a 404 error for invalid IDs
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Test the route with valid and invalid user IDs
editorial.dryRun: Route should return the corresponding user details for valid IDs and a 404 error for invalid IDs
